sglang.title: Add GLM45 tool interruption support
sglang.author: Leoyzen
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR adds support for GLM-4.5 models where tool calls can interrupt reasoning blocks without an explicit closing tag.
Specifically, GLM-4.5 uses the pattern <|think|>...<|tool|> where the occurrence of the tool start token acts as an implicit terminator for the reasoning block. Previou

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
grok2 model npu adapt
Modifications


Grok2 employs softcap attention, but the existing attention operators in Ascend do not support softcap. To enable its functionality, we extended the native code of Torch's scaled_dot_product_attention to include softcap support
On NPU, the current rop

sglang.title: [HiCache] fix compatibility bugs with eagle and HiCacheStorage
sglang.author: huangtingwei9988
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
EAGLE uses bigram keys in its radix tree, for example, [1, 2, 3, 4] -> [(1,2), (2,3), (3,4)]. Therefore, when prefetching kvcache from storage, token_ids also need to be converted into bigram keys. Otherwise, the hash values ​​will not match, and the cache will not be hit.
Modifications

sglang.title: Fix CompressedTensorsMxInt4MoE abstract method and relax GPQA baseline
sglang.author: alisonshao
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Rename apply → apply_weights in CompressedTensorsMxInt4MoE to match the abstract method in CompressedTensorsMoEScheme/BaseMoEScheme. Without this, instantiation raises TypeError: Can't instantiate abstract class CompressedTensorsMxInt4MoE with abstract method apply_weights.
Lower GPQA_BASEL

sglang.title: feat: Add FP8 KV cache support for Triton attention backend
sglang.author: zack041
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
The Triton attention backend does not correctly handle FP8 KV cache. It does not pass k_scale/v_scale to set_kv_buffer, causing scales to default to 1.0. In addition, the Triton kernels lack dequantization support. Unlike FlashInfer which handles scales internally, the Triton kernels rece

vllm.title: [CI] Defining extended V1 e2e + engine tests
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The V1 e2e tests (including test_spec_decode.py) currently only run in 1-GPU pools. Tests decorated with @multi_gpu_only(num_gpus=2) and @multi_gpu_marks(num_gpus=4) silently skip because only one GPU is available. This means the following tests have no CI coverage:

test_draft_model_tensor_parallel

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The fix prevents vLLM from using the CPU shared-memory communicator unless all ranks in that TP/PP group agree on the exact same SHM group name. This PR resolves the following issue: #33628.
TLDR:

If ranks are truly on the same node, they compute the same name -> use fast CPUSHM.

vllm.title: Fix unresolved-import errors when using Astral's ty by removing src.root
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Setting[tool.ty.src] root = "./vllm" sets the first-party module search path to ./vllm/. This means import vllm.config looks for ./vllm/vllm/config/, which doesn't exist. This causes cross-module imports using the vllm. prefix to fail with unresolved-import errors.
From talking to @hmellor src.root

vllm.author: yashwantbezawada
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #29608
When using async KV connectors with layerwise operations (wait_for_layer_load, save_kv_layer), these calls are made inside the @maybe_transfer_kv_layer decorator. During CUDA graph replay, only the captured GPU ops run - the decorator's Python code is skipped entirely. This mean

vllm.title: [ci] Add Ray compatibility check informational CI job
vllm.author: jeffreywang88
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Add an informational CI step that checks whether Ray LLM can generate lock files compatible with the current vllm's dependency constraints, catching conflicts early.
The script fetches Ray's requirement files, extracts the installed vllm's deps (including audio extras), and runs uv pip comp

vllm.author: robertgshaw2-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

DeepSeekV2FusedQkvAProj.forward had a data-dependent conditional 0 < num_tokens <= 16 that breaks torch.compile because it creates control flow that depends on tensor shape at trace time
Wraps the kernel dispatch (dsv3_fused_a_gemm vs F.linear) in a direct_register_custom_op, making it opaq

Forcing default backend to 'trtllm' for stability.

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix backslash-continuation in pytest marker re-quoting and treat exit code 5 as success
vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow-up on:

#35170

Two issues with run-amd-test.sh:

Backslash line-continuations break -m marker re-quoting. Commands like:

   pytest -v -s models/language/generation \
     -m hybrid_model \
     --num-shards=2

get flattened to tokens where \ survives as a standalone word. The re_quote_pytes

Purpose
Add GPUDirect RDMA support for XPU in NIXL connector.
Requirements：

UCX must include the fix from openucx/ucx#11187.

Limitations:

Must be set UCX_NET_DEVICES manually for better performance until openucx/ucx#11180 is merged.
Currently ze-ipc is not supported. openucx/ucx#11218

vllm.title: [Model Runner V2][Perf] align dummy_run tokens to uniform decode for dp cudagraph
vllm.author: izhuhaoran
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
When using data parallelism (DP) with CUDA graphs and speculative decoding, execute_dummy_batch calls _dummy_run(1, uniform_decode=True) on idle DP ranks. Previously, _dummy_run always split tokens without considering uniform_decode_query_len, producing a token count (e.g., 1) that doesn't m

vllm.title: [Tool Parser] Fix Qwen3Coder streaming parameter loss with speculative decode
vllm.author: voipmonitor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fixes a critical bug where the Qwen3Coder streaming tool parser silently drops parameters when speculative decoding is enabled (e.g. num_speculative_tokens=5). This causes downstream clients to receive tool calls with missing fields — for example a write tool call arrives as {"filePath": "/p

vllm.title: [Core] Add optional flags to check for repetitive token patterns in engine output
vllm.author: aykoppol
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR allows users to end generation early if they see repetitive token patterns (for example, nonsensically repeated sentences) in their output. This is a real failure case for models prone to hallucination. Testing with Qwen3 VL on multimodal input shows a ~33% increase in throughput and
